column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: -99% ice cover along reach between lake outlet and gauge. Downstream of gauge CIC.
column14: -FT2 wading measurement -Measured at open section just below lake outlet, about 8km NE of gauge. -@ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ -Cut taken in line with gauge, usual spot. -Initial hole was dry, suspect hit the top of a boulder. Second hole was 3-4m toward river middle away from first. -No reset due to surge and difficulty in seeing cut clearly. Also stage is fluctuating dramaticlly day to day. Seemed best to leave as is. -MGH calculation from all 5 minute readings between start/end. Average was same as mean time so just entered that. Mid-section
HG: 7.316 Surge: 0.000  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.  No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream

-Complete ice cover entire visible reach around gauge.
-Open at lake outlet/mmt area as usual but water over rocks which have been dry past few years.Thin ice crust over this section.
column14: -FT2 wading measurement at lake outlet, ~7km upstream of gauge -moved down ~20m from usual transect at this area, as water is flowing under ice at usual area went downstream to area that was open bank to bank. -MGH from limited points. Trace steady. Same as average of all 5min readings. -@ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ -DWL taken through ice, in line with gauge, ~5m from shore -no reset, did not have time to run confirmation levels. -existing SRC from last visit 0.011m, effective drift is -0.006m Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Mostly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Cross Stream
column13:
-Open water conditions
-Few boulders showing in channel but most submerged
column14: -Original attempt with FT2 unsuccessful due to low SNR, total was significantly lower than expected and velocities recorded did not seem reasonable. -Measured with StreamPro in trimaran and measurement looks good.  -intensity levels were high with streampro so not sure where the low SNR readings issue is from in FT2, appears to reflect equipment vs. river conditions. -although not within 5% of curve, suspect this is mostly to do with lack of consistentcy in where levels are taken. No time to add third measurement. -stationary moving bed / no compass cal performed -@ Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ -Two cuts taken. One in line with BM cluster and one in line with gauge, where line enters water and close to block. 0.013m drop between the two. Future SRC's should be consistently taken in line with the gauge to avoid SRC errors from slope of river at station ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Moderate Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: -Running in a small amount of grass and grass above water bent and flattened about 0.3-0.4m above water level. No obvious high water mark found.
column14: -Man to Man tethered boat mmt about 30m upstream of gauge -compass cal had several issues that could not be resolved on site. Calibration exceeds QA/QC standard. -cobble and boulder river bed, moving bed should be minimal if present as detected with the MBT even though it has warnings. -Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value -DWL taken where line enters the water  ADCP by Moving Boat
